input_row.title: Ensure that 'Unattached disks' are encrypted with 'Customer Managed Key' (CMK)
input_row.description: refer to Azure documentation to delete unattached da..."
input_row.impact: If data stored in the disk is no longer useful, refer to Azure documentation to delete unattached data disks at: -https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/compute/disks/delete -https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/disk?view=azure-cli-latest#az- disk-delete If data stored in the disk is important, to encrypt the disk refer to azure documentation at: -https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/virtual-machines/disks-enable- customer-managed-keys-portal -https://docs.microsoft.com/en- us/rest/api/compute/disks/update#encryptionsettings Default Value: By default, managed disks are encrypted with a Platform-managed key. References: 1. https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security/fundamentals/azure-disk- encryption-vms-vmss 2. https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/security-center/security-center-disk- encryption?toc=%2fazure%2fsecurity%2ftoc.json 3. https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/rest/api/compute/disks/delete 4. https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/disk?view=azure-cli-latest#az-disk- delete 5. https://docs.microsoft.com/en- us/rest/api/compute/disks/update#encryptionsettings 6. https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/cli/azure/disk?view=azure-cli-latest#az-disk- update 7. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/mcsb-data- protection#dp-5-use-customer-managed-key-option-in-data-at-rest-encryption- when-required CIS Controls:

gpt_response:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The control focuses on verifying that unattached disks in Azure are encrypted with customer-managed keys (CMK). 
- Given that encryption settings, particularly the 'keySource' property, are standard Azure resource properties and can be queried via Azure APIs, the audit can be fully automated.

**PROGRAM NAME:** azure_compute_disk_encryption_verified

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- This program would use Azure Resource Manager APIs or Azure CLI commands to check the encryption status of unattached disks.
- The specific resources to enumerate would be unattached managed disks within a subscription or resource group.
- The key properties to validate will include:
  - `encryptionSettings.enabled`: Should be true
  - `encryptionSettings.keySource`: Should be set to 'Microsoft.Keyvault' to indicate CMK usage.
- The criteria for a Pass/Fail verdict will be:
  - Pass: `encryptionSettings.enabled` is true AND `encryptionSettings.keySource` is 'Microsoft.Keyvault'.
  - Fail: Either of the properties do not meet the expected criteria.
- Example API endpoint or CLI command to check the disk's encryption settings: 
  - API: `GET https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/{subscription-id}/resourceGroups/{resource-group}/providers/Microsoft.Compute/disks/{disk-name}?api-version=2021-03-01`
  - CLI: `az disk show --name <disk-name> --resource-group <resource-group> --query "encryptionSettings"`
